definition: Aging electorates systematically redirect government spending from agricultural R&D (which has a 20:1 benefit-cost ratio) to healthcare, creating a slow-moving food security degradation. This is itself a demographic fiscal choice — the aging political economy directly undermines long-term agricultural productivity.
evidence: Aging electorates redirect spending to healthcare
open_questions: Can private sector R&D compensate for declining public investment?
Is the crowding out effect quantifiable across OECD?
How long is the lag between R&D spending decline and productivity impact?
